jokes.text: You always ruin everything
jokes.setup: Liz approaches Jack to talk
jokes.punchline: Immediate hostile response for no apparent reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character work - captures their dynamic in four words

jokes.text: An eagle with the head of a bear
jokes.setup: Asked what animal describes Jack
jokes.punchline: Impossible hybrid animal that sounds intimidating
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist imagery that perfectly captures Jack's impossible nature - highly quotable

jokes.text: You hired someone to investigate yourself? That's weird
jokes.setup: Jack explains hiring PI to investigate himself
jokes.punchline: Liz's understated reaction to obvious weirdness
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man work from Liz, pointing out the obvious absurdity

jokes.text: Well, you do the same thing with your therapist every week, don't you?
jokes.setup: Liz calls Jack's self-investigation weird
jokes.punchline: Jack compares it to therapy in perfectly logical Jack fashion
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - Jack's ability to rationalize anything with corporate logic

jokes.text: Wait a minute. That's a fake phone
jokes.setup: Jack takes what appears to be a phone call
jokes.punchline: Liz realizes mid-conversation that the phone is fake
jokes.explanation: Classic visual gag with good timing on the reveal

jokes.text: Bling, bling! That is ghetto fabulous
jokes.setup: Angie shows off expensive jewelry
jokes.punchline: Liz awkwardly using dated slang
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Liz's awkwardness with different cultures

jokes.text: This belonged to Brooke Astor
jokes.setup: Showing off expensive jewelry
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to dead socialite's belongings
jokes.explanation: Good character detail establishing Angie's wealth and connections

jokes.text: I'm whipped. Angie got me up at 7:30 today
jokes.setup: Tracy admitting he's controlled by his wife
jokes.punchline: 7:30 AM being presented as unreasonably early
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character work - his complete disconnect from normal life

jokes.text: Did you know in the morning they have food, TV, almost everything. It's pretty good
jokes.setup: Tracy being surprised by early wake-up
jokes.punchline: Discovering that mornings exist and have amenities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on Tracy's lifestyle - perfectly executed character comedy

jokes.text: Tracy's like a horny child. He needs constant adult supervision
jokes.setup: Angie explaining why she needs help with Tracy
jokes.punchline: Comparing grown man to horny child
jokes.explanation: Sharp character observation with uncomfortable accuracy

jokes.text: Oh, you looking for a sassy black friend? Well, you got one now, girlfriend
jokes.setup: Liz expressing enthusiasm about working with Angie
jokes.punchline: Angie calling out the trope directly
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on TV tropes while advancing character relationships

jokes.text: Putting on this weight is the best thing that ever happened to me
jokes.setup: Jenna receiving fan mail
jokes.punchline: Weight gain being her career breakthrough
jokes.explanation: Good character development and Hollywood satire

jokes.text: Enorme. Make him chase the chunk
jokes.setup: Commercial for plus-size perfume
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous slogan combining romance with fat jokes
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted fake commercial with incredible economy and quotability

jokes.text: Can't plus size women wear regular perfume?
jokes.setup: Watching the Enorme commercial
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the obvious absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man observation highlighting the commercial's absurdity

jokes.text: I just like you more when you're fat
jokes.setup: Jack praising Jenna's career success
jokes.punchline: Casually cruel personal observation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly brutal Jack line with excellent economy and character truth

jokes.text: The 1976 Democratic National Convention. But it's okay, I was there beating up hippies
jokes.setup: PI asking about arrests
jokes.punchline: Violence at political event presented as positive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character work - turning criminal activity into conservative virtue

jokes.text: My brother Eddie sells faulty sprinkler systems to elementary schools
jokes.setup: PI asking about family skeletons
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning endangering children
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing the Donaghy family's casual corruption

jokes.text: My mother's an Olympic level racist
jokes.setup: Continuing family skeleton list
jokes.punchline: Describing racism with athletic achievement terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect economy - comparing prejudice to athletic achievement

jokes.text: Unless getting thrown out of a Chili's is a crime
jokes.setup: Describing drunk family members
jokes.punchline: Specific restaurant chain as pinnacle of family shame
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail that grounds the absurdity in relatable reality

jokes.text: You know what? Don't tell me. I like to find that out on my own. I'm a watcher
jokes.setup: PI asking about weird sex stuff
jokes.punchline: Creepy admission about voyeuristic tendencies
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that adds disturbing dimension to side character

jokes.text: If I sign a friend up at my gym, I get a free hat
jokes.setup: Ending serious business meeting
jokes.punchline: Awkward gym membership sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail showing professional/personal boundary confusion

jokes.text: The offer to play Ms. Pac-Man in the live action Atari movie
jokes.setup: Jenna listing career opportunities
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous movie concept treated seriously
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist Hollywood satire with specific video game reference

jokes.text: A high five. It was our special thing
jokes.setup: Describing secret ritual with Jack
jokes.punchline: Most common gesture in world presented as intimate bond
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna delusion - treating mundane interaction as profound connection

jokes.text: You can't ask a tiger not to turn back into a Chinese dude at midnight
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his natural impulses with animal metaphors
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical transformation reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy absurdism - perfect escalation from reasonable metaphors to complete nonsense

jokes.text: The only way I could feel better about myself is to get booby-slapped by a coked-out Russian stripper
jokes.setup: Tracy feeling bad about betraying Liz
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and disturbing method of self-comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character work - turning guilt into bizarre sexual scenario

jokes.text: Me want food, right?
jokes.setup: Jack questioning if Jenna still has eating problem
jokes.punchline: Jenna doing bad caveman impression to prove hunger
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Jenna's desperate performance of hunger

jokes.text: I'm gonna fatten you up, grow to love you, and then my uncles'll slit your throat
jokes.setup: Kenneth comparing helping Jenna to pig farm experience
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of farm animal slaughter process
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark Kenneth moment - innocent delivery of disturbing farm reality

jokes.text: He's the King of Spain, Jack. I don't think that counts
jokes.setup: Jack defending country club diversity with Johnny Carlos
jokes.punchline: PI pointing out that royalty doesn't count as regular diversity
jokes.explanation: Good class/privilege humor showing Jack's disconnect from real diversity

jokes.text: I could never pay an English-speaking person that little. They'd starve
jokes.setup: Jack defending undocumented employee
jokes.punchline: Admission that wages are inhumanly low
jokes.explanation: Sharp economic satire showing how exploitation depends on desperation

jokes.text: You don't have a massive collection of cookie jars, do you?
jokes.setup: PI wrapping up seemingly clean investigation
jokes.punchline: Unexpected specific question about innocent hobby
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - harmless hobby as major scandal setup

jokes.text: Yes, I'm Victor Nightingale
jokes.setup: Award ceremony for cookie jar collecting
jokes.punchline: Jack using embarrassing pseudonym
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Jack's secret shame about his hobby

jokes.text: That's a fake! Giuliani doesn't collect dolls
jokes.setup: PI showing photo of Giuliani with doll collection
jokes.punchline: Jack's immediate defensive denial
jokes.explanation: Good reaction showing Jack's desperation to maintain dignity

jokes.text: Because he incinerated them! In 1989, the year he ran for mayor
jokes.setup: Jack calling photo fake
jokes.punchline: Giuliani destroying hobby for political advancement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing extreme lengths politicians go for image

jokes.text: He went to the vet. His cobra got sick
jokes.setup: Angie asking where Tracy is
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of pet cobra veterinary care
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about Tracy's exotic lifestyle

jokes.text: Then my thumb got caught in my butt, so I nodded my head until it came out
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining what he did at vet
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical physical scenario
jokes.explanation: Peak Tracy absurdism - impossible anatomy treated as mechanical problem

jokes.text: You smell like Enorme and brass polish
jokes.setup: Angie detecting Tracy's strip club visit
jokes.punchline: Specific scent combination revealing location
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to earlier product placement with logical detection method

jokes.text: Thank you for being my secretary!
jokes.setup: Tracy saying goodbye to Liz
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstanding their professional relationship
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character work showing his complete cluelessness about hierarchy

jokes.text: He's an entrepreneur. What's the character's name? Slick-back Lamar
jokes.setup: Angie claiming Tracy isn't playing a pimp but an entrepreneur
jokes.punchline: Character name that obviously confirms he's a pimp
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reveal showing Angie's contradictory position

jokes.text: Did you just try to control my body with your white hand?
jokes.setup: Physical gesture from Liz
jokes.punchline: Angie interprets innocent gesture through racial lens
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered as character conflict - shows how racial dynamics complicate simple interactions

jokes.text: We open on a lone soldier walking through the desert. The year 1861. The place Mars
jokes.setup: Grizz pitching sketch idea
jokes.punchline: Historical period piece on another planet
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist sketch concept with perfect period/sci-fi contradiction

jokes.text: It made my dad's heart just up and give out. And that's what made my mom's friend Ron move in
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing fattening food effects
jokes.punchline: Father's death leading to mother's relationship
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth dark backstory delivered with innocent tone

jokes.text: You're like John Kerry with his windsurfing. I warned him too
jokes.setup: Jack refusing to give up cookie jars
jokes.punchline: PI claiming to have advised major political figure
jokes.explanation: Good political reference showing how elite hobbies damage campaigns

jokes.text: Tell her you like her nails. Always works for me at the bank
jokes.setup: Liz asking for advice about angry black woman
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's awkward cross-cultural interaction strategy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Kenneth's misguided social strategies

jokes.text: Usually, but now you got me thinking about it.
jokes.setup: Asked if he can walk and talk
jokes.punchline: Simple question makes Kenneth self-conscious about basic function
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character beat - overthinks simple things when attention is drawn to them

jokes.text: Women with low self-esteem take refuge in either food or sex. Not me, of course
jokes.setup: Liz explaining psychology to Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction revealing her own issues
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware character moment with perfect contradiction timing

jokes.text: Who picked out your outfit? Stevie Wonder?
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to be mean to make herself eat
jokes.punchline: Dated disability-based insult
jokes.explanation: Weak insult comedy that relies on disability stereotype - fits character but not clever

jokes.text: Smooth move, Exlax
jokes.setup: Kenneth learning to be mean
jokes.punchline: Extremely dated insult delivered innocently
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Kenneth's outdated reference points

jokes.text: I've always viewed it as an upside down 'wow'
jokes.setup: Liz pointing out cookie jar says 'mom'
jokes.punchline: Jack's elaborate psychological denial
jokes.explanation: Brilliant psychological defense mechanism delivered with perfect Jack confidence

jokes.text: Every time I meet a new person I figure out how I'm gonna fight 'em
jokes.setup: Jack giving Liz fighting advice
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of constant violence assessment
jokes.explanation: Great character revelation showing Jack's alpha male psychology

jokes.text: You have a gimpy left knee, right?
jokes.setup: Jack explaining he figures out how to fight everyone he meets
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates by noting Liz's weakness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's strategic mindset extends to physical assessment of friends

jokes.text: Oh, daddy! Go on, keep talking. What else don't you like about me?
jokes.setup: Kenneth insulting Jenna to make her eat
jokes.punchline: Jenna getting sexually aroused by abuse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy revealing Jenna's psychological issues with perfect timing

jokes.text: Also, your nails look tacky
jokes.setup: Liz confronting Angie using Kenneth's advice
jokes.punchline: Awkward deployment of nail compliment as insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Liz's misapplication of Kenneth's advice

jokes.text: Also, your nails look tacky.
jokes.setup: Liz confronting Angie
jokes.punchline: Uses Tracy's advice about complimenting nails but as insult instead
jokes.explanation: Good callback that subverts Tracy's earlier advice while escalating the conflict appropriately

jokes.text: But I don't care. 'Cause I'm putting my foot down.
jokes.setup: Tracy admitting he's immature but taking control
jokes.punchline: Uses childish phrase while claiming maturity
jokes.explanation: Appropriate character irony showing Tracy's limited emotional vocabulary even when being serious

jokes.text: But the both of you, you're forcing me to act like an adult. An adult!
jokes.setup: Tracy breaking up fight between Liz and Angie
jokes.punchline: Treating adulthood as horrible burden
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character work - childish celebrity forced into maturity

jokes.text: Well, it turns out she's the wrong kind of crazy.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining why he couldn't get Jenna to eat
jokes.punchline: Clinical assessment of Jenna's mental state as wrong type for his purposes
jokes.explanation: Perfectly innocent Kenneth delivering brutal character assessment with farm-boy practicality

jokes.text: They'd go into the bedroom to sort out their paperwork.
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing mom's friend Ron's visits
jokes.punchline: Innocent euphemism for obvious sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence using euphemism that reveals his childhood naivety about adult relationships

jokes.text: It was almost as if I took every problem that I ever had and I put it inside that cookie jar. And I sealed it up so tight that nothing would never, ever, ever get out
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing childhood cookie jar memory
jokes.punchline: Dark psychological coping mechanism described innocently
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment revealing Kenneth's psychological damage with perfect innocent delivery

jokes.text: And I sealed it up so tight that nothing would never, ever, ever get out.
jokes.setup: Kenneth describing putting problems in imaginary cookie jar
jokes.punchline: Overly emphatic description of psychological repression
jokes.explanation: Dark psychology delivered with Kenneth's innocent intensity, revealing deeper trauma beneath surface

jokes.text: I'd give cookie jars about a 'B.'
jokes.setup: Deep psychological revelation about cookie jars as trauma containers
jokes.punchline: Reduces profound emotional significance to casual grade
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan after emotional revelation - perfect Kenneth character comedy with excellent structure and timing

jokes.text: Please tell Patty and Beth and all of them that, uh, Vic Nightingale says hello
jokes.setup: Jack giving Kenneth his cookie jar collection
jokes.punchline: Maintaining cookie jar community connections despite giving up hobby
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's genuine attachment to hobby community

jokes.text: You son of a bitch. You son of a bitch
jokes.setup: Jack seeing his cookie jars being destroyed despite giving them away
jokes.punchline: Repeated frustrated curse at betrayal
jokes.explanation: Solid emotional payoff showing Jack's genuine pain at loss
